content.claim: Hungary has the highest level of segregation of students from less and more advantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools, and segregation of the most and least disadvantaged students was highest in the EU in 2022 PISA
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Segregation is multifactorial; direction of causation between tracking policy and segregation levels is not definitively established
Does not isolate contribution of 6/8-year tracks to overall segregation
Geographic/settlement factors documented as independent drivers of segregation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
